content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree containing 2N2N2N vertices and 2N‚àí12N-12N‚àí1 undirected edges. Vertex uuu has the color AuA_uAu‚Äã.

It is guaranteed that each color from 111 to NNN appears **exactly twice** in the tree.

Process QQQ queries of two type on this tree:


1  u  v1 \ \ u \ \ v1  u  v: Swap the colors on vertices uuu and vvv, i.e, swap AuA_uAu‚Äã and AvA_vAv‚Äã.
2  k2\ \ k2  k: Let uuu and vvv be the two vertices colored kkk.

Suppose all vertices on the simple path from uuu to vvv are blocked.

Count the number of pairs of vertices (x,y)(x, y)(x,y) such that x<yx \lt yx<y, Ax=AyA_x = A_yAx‚Äã=Ay‚Äã, and the simple path from xxx to yyy does not contain any blocked vertices.

More simply, count the number of paths between vertices of the same color that do not pass through any blocked vertices.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ ‚Äî the number of distinct values in the tree and the number of queries, respectively.
The next 2N‚àí12 N-12N‚àí1 lines describe the edges. The iii-th of these 2N‚àí12 N-12N‚àí1 lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui‚Äã and viv_ivi‚Äã, denoting an edge between uiu_iui‚Äã and viv_ivi‚Äã.
The next line contains 2N2 N2N space separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,A2NA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_{2 N}A1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,A2N‚Äã.
The next QQQ lines contain the queries:

For a type 111 query, the line contains three space-separated integers 111, uuu, and vvv.
For a type 222 query, the line contains two space-separated integers 222 and kkk.






Output Format
For each test case, output the answer for each query of type 222 on a new line.
constraints: The input graph describes a tree.
Each element between 111 and NNN appears exactly twice in AAA.
For queries of type 111: 1‚â§u,v‚â§2N1 \leq u, v \leq 2N1‚â§u,v‚â§2N
For queries of type 222: 1‚â§k‚â§N1 \leq k \leq N1‚â§k‚â§N
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.
The sum of QQQ over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

For the first query, vertices {2,3}\{2, 3\}{2,3} are blocked.
There is no pair of nodes having the same value such that the path connecting them doesn't contain a blocked vertex; so the answer is 000.
After the second query, AAA becomes [1,1,2,2][1, 1, 2, 2][1,1,2,2].
For the third query, vertices {1,2}\{1, 2\}{1,2} are blocked.
The pair (3,4)(3, 4)(3,4) satisfies the conditions, so the answer is 111.

Test case 222:

For the first query, vertices {2,5,6,7}\{2, 5, 6, 7\}{2,5,6,7} are blocked.
There is no pair of nodes having the same value such that the path connecting them doesn't contain a blocked vertex; so the answer is 000.
After the second query, AAA becomes [3,4,3,4,1,2,1,2][3, 4, 3, 4, 1, 2, 1, 2][3,4,3,4,1,2,1,2].
For the third query, vertices {2,5,6,7}\{2, 5, 6, 7\}{2,5,6,7} are blocked.
The only valid pair is (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3).
After the fourth query, AAA becomes [3,4,3,1,4,2,1,2][3, 4, 3, 1, 4, 2, 1, 2][3,4,3,1,4,2,1,2].
For the fifth query, vertices {2,5}\{2, 5\}{2,5} are blocked.
Valid pairs are (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3) and (6,8)(6, 8)(6,8), so the answer is 222.
For the sixth query, vertices {2,4,7,8}\{2, 4, 7, 8\}{2,4,7,8} are blocked.
The only valid pair is (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3).
